issuesFixed.title: Add role='region' to swiper-parent div
issuesFixed.description: The carousel region was missing the semantic role='region' attribute required by WAI-ARIA carousel pattern

issuesFixed.title: Add accessible name via aria-labelledby to swiper-parent div
issuesFixed.description: The carousel region was missing an accessible name. Added aria-labelledby pointing to the heading element ID

filesModified.before: <div class="swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1" aria-roledescription="<?php esc_attr_e( 'carousel', 'takt' ); ?>">
filesModified.after: <div class="swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1" role="region" aria-roledescription="<?php esc_attr_e( 'carousel', 'takt' ); ?>" aria-labelledby="<?php echo esc_attr( theme_block_region_id() . '-heading' ); ?>">

attributesAdded.name: role
attributesAdded.purpose: Identifies the carousel as a landmark region for assistive technology users

attributesAdded.name: aria-labelledby
attributesAdded.purpose: Provides accessible name by referencing the carousel's heading element ID

phpTemplate.note: The aria-labelledby value uses theme_block_region_id() which generates a unique ID for the block instance, with '-heading' suffix to reference the ThemeHeading component's ID

attributesAdded.name: role
attributesAdded.purpose: Identifies the carousel as a landmark region in the editor

attributesAdded.name: aria-labelledby
attributesAdded.purpose: Provides accessible name by referencing the heading element ID (uses clientId in editor context)

tsxEditor.note: The aria-labelledby value uses clientId (Gutenberg block ID) with '-heading' suffix, matching the ThemeHeading component's ID pattern in the editor

waiAriaCompliance.before: Partial compliance - had aria-roledescription but missing role and accessible name
waiAriaCompliance.after: Full WAI-ARIA carousel pattern compliance - role='region' + aria-roledescription='carousel' + aria-labelledby

screenReaderExperience.before: Screen readers would not announce the carousel as a region and would lack context about what the carousel contains
screenReaderExperience.after: Screen readers will announce 'Gallery Carousel region' (or custom heading text) when entering the carousel, providing clear context

keyboardNavigation.impact: role='region' makes the carousel discoverable via landmark navigation shortcuts (e.g., NVDA+D in NVDA, Ctrl+Option+U in VoiceOver)

phpSyntax.method: Build completed successfully without PHP errors

tsxSyntax.method: Build completed successfully without TypeScript errors

parity.note: PHP and TSX have identical ARIA attributes on swiper-parent div

build.warnings: Only pre-existing performance warnings (asset size limits)

steps: 1. Open test page in browser with screen reader (NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver)
2. Use landmark navigation shortcut (NVDA+D, Insert+Ctrl+L, or Ctrl+Option+U)
3. Verify carousel is listed as a region with accessible name
5. Verify screen reader announces the heading text as the region name
1. Inspect swiper-parent div in browser DevTools
3. Verify aria-labelledby attribute points to heading element ID
4. Verify the referenced heading element exists and has matching ID
5. Test with multiple carousel instances on the same page to ensure unique IDs
1. Open a test page in WordPress editor
3. Inspect the editor HTML in browser DevTools
4. Verify swiper-parent div has role='region' and aria-labelledby attributes
5. Verify aria-labelledby references the heading element with clientId-based ID
1. Test a carousel variation with no heading (empty heading field)
2. Verify the section has fallback aria-label='Gallery Carousel'
3. Note: The aria-labelledby will still reference a heading ID, but if heading is empty, the section's aria-label provides fallback context

notes: Both role='region' and aria-labelledby are critical for WAI-ARIA carousel pattern compliance
The aria-labelledby approach is preferred over aria-label because it references actual content (the heading), providing better context and automatic updates when heading changes
The theme_block_region_id() function in PHP and clientId in TSX ensure unique IDs for multiple carousel instances on the same page
PHP and TSX implementations are perfectly in sync - identical attribute structure
Build completed successfully with no new warnings or errors

relatedDocumentation.requirement: role='region' and accessible name (via aria-label or aria-labelledby)
Semantic structure must be programmatically determinable
All UI components must have accessible names
